title: Direct Simulation of LiNi0.8Mn0.1Co0.1O2 Transport Properties Using an Efficient and Accurate Machine Learning Potential
core_claim: A fine-tuned MACE foundation model plus active learning can build a reliable machine-learning potential for NMC811 that reproduces DFT accuracy and enables direct molecular-dynamics simulation of lithium self-diffusion and diffusion coefficients at larger scales.
triage_rationale: This is a concrete materials workflow paper, not just a benchmark: it uses a foundation model, active learning, and direct MD simulation to answer a physically meaningful transport question in NMC811. The topic is narrow but methodologically substantive, with clear applied relevance and enough technical content to merit review.
likely_red_flags: Abstract does not report quantitative diffusion results or validation details, so the strength of the claimed accuracy is unclear from triage alone.
The foundation model signal is applied in a specialized materials pipeline, but the novelty may be incremental if the main contribution is a tuned surrogate for existing DFT workflows.
